title: OpenStack Nova Compute Instance Build and Spawn Failure Rate
exclusions: This UC is OpenStack Nova build, scheduling, and compute-side spawn-stage log evidence in `index=openstack` (file tails, oslo JSON, or journald-filtered) with the sourcetype OR and coalesce / regex work in the `spl` field. UC-19.1.45 (RabbitMQ Management / Erlang file logs) covers oslo RPC queue and mem_alarm / partition class messages on the bus. UC-19.1.46 (Galera / MariaDB wsrep poll and mysqld error logs) covers quorum and SST/IST stories. A green result here does not prove Rabbit or SQL are healthy, and a red result here does not replace those sibling detectors during a region-wide 500. oVirt VDSM / Engine and KVM spawn outside OpenStack (future UC-19.1.41–43-style) are out of scope. Cinder-only or Neutron-only UCs for attach or port work without a nova-scoped line are not covered. Glance upload or signature flows without a boot request, Keystone general token-rate problems without a spawn-adjacent string, Heat stack failures, Octavia LB builds, Magnum COE, Trove DBaaS, Manila NFS, Designate DNS, and public-cloud IaaS with no access to your oslo files are all separate stories. The UC also does not replace Prometheus placement inventory or Ceilometer for raw VCPU math—it answers “are spawn-stage failures spiking, for what class, and on which host,” not “how many VCPU are free before lunch.” Sibling UCs exist so you can page the Rabbit owner and the DBA with the right playbook without pretending nova regex is the only signal.
dataSources: `index=openstack` with a sourcetype OR union that matches what your site actually ingests, not a fake name per error string. Usual sources: the umbrella `openstack:nova`, or split stanzas `openstack:nova:compute`, `openstack:nova:scheduler`, `openstack:nova:conductor`, `openstack:nova:api` when `inputs.conf` points each `monitor://` at a file; optional `oslo:json` when the body is JSON; optional `linux:journald` when you only have systemd’s journal. The OpenStack add-on for Splunk on Splunkbase 2905 is archived—do not bet a greenfield on it. Prefer Universal Forwarder, `props.conf` tuned for oslo.log and Python stack traces, and a src_kind discriminator in SPL. Field names vary: request_id, instance_uuid vs `instance` vs `instance_id`, level vs log_level. Sample five real events and confirm your spellings before you lock the saved search.
app: Universal Forwarder 9.2+ (or a supported log shipper) on OpenStack control-plane and compute nodes, plus a small app such as `TA_openstack_nova_uf` for `local/inputs.conf` and `local/props.conf`. Kolla-Ansible, TripleO, Charmed OpenStack, and Red Hat OpenStack Platform (OSP) all change paths and container boundaries; the literal examples in Step 1 cover /var/log/nova and a /var/log/kolla/nova style mount. The archived add-on 2905 can remain in a lab, but the primary story here is file tails plus parsing you own.
spl:
index=openstack (sourcetype="openstack:nova" OR sourcetype="openstack:nova:compute" OR sourcetype="openstack:nova:scheduler" OR sourcetype="openstack:nova:conductor" OR sourcetype="openstack:nova:api" OR sourcetype="oslo:json" OR (sourcetype="linux:journald" (Unit=*nova* OR _raw="*nova-compute*" OR _raw="*nova-scheduler*" OR _raw="*nova-conductor*" OR _raw="*nova-api*" OR _raw="*nova-wsgi*"))) earliest=-1h
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype="openstack:nova:compute","compute", sourcetype="openstack:nova:scheduler","scheduler", sourcetype="openstack:nova:conductor","conductor", sourcetype="openstack:nova:api","api", match(sourcetype,"^openstack:nova$"),"umbrella", sourcetype="oslo:json" AND match(_raw,"(?i)nova(\-|[.])"),"oslo_nova", sourcetype="linux:journald","journald", 1=1,"other")
| eval msg=coalesce(message, body, _raw, msg, payload)
| eval lv=lower(tostring(coalesce(level, log_level, severity, priority, "")))
| eval r1=if(isnotnull(request_id), tostring(request_id), null())
| eval r2=if(isnotnull(requestId), tostring(requestId), null())
| eval r3=if(isnotnull(request_uuid), tostring(request_uuid), null())
| eval iid=tostring(coalesce(instance_uuid, inst_uuid, instance_id, instance, ""))
| rex field=msg max_match=1 "(?<reqid_r>req-[0-9a-fA-F-]{8,64})"
| eval request_id=coalesce(r1, r2, r3, reqid_r, "")
| eval is_nvh=if(match(msg, "(?i)NoValidHost|NoMoreHosts|no valid host|No more (valid )?hosts with capacity"),1,0)
| eval is_build_abort=if(match(msg, "(?i)BuildAbortException|Failed to spawn( instance)?|failed to (build|spawn)|SpawnFailed|BuildError"),1,0)
| eval is_resched=if(match(msg, "(?i)RescheduledException|re-?schedul|rescheduling( exception)?|max scheduling attempts|retry on different host"),1,0)
| eval is_cinder=if(match(msg, "(?i)CinderConnectionFailed|VolumeNotFound|VolumeAttachFailed|volume.*(attach|connection).*fail|is not yet available on the right storage"),1,0)
| eval is_neut=if(match(msg, "(?i)PortBindingFailed|NoSubnetsAvailable|NetworkNotFound|VIF.*failed|Neutron(nova)?(Exception|Error)|binding failed"),1,0)
| eval is_glance=if(match(msg, "(?i)ImageNotAuthorized|ImageNotFound|image.*(inactive|unavailable|403)|Glance.*HTTP\s*5[0-9x]|image.*(fetch|download).*fail"),1,0)
| eval is_ks=if(match(msg, "(?i)HTTP\s*401|Unauthorized(\s*\()?|keystone(\S|\s{1,2})(auth|error)|No valid token|invalid token( cache)?|identity.*(401|unauthorized)"),1,0)
| eval is_api5=if(match(msg, "(?i)(/v2\.1/servers|/os-server-groups).*HTTP\s*5[0-9][0-9]"),1,0)
| eval failure_class=case(is_nvh=1, "no_valid_host", is_build_abort=1, "build_abort", is_resched=1, "reschedule", is_cinder=1, "cinder_attach", is_neut=1, "neutron_port", is_glance=1, "glance_image", is_ks=1, "keystone_auth", is_api5=1, "api_http5xx", 1=1, "unclassified")
| where (is_nvh+is_build_abort+is_resched+is_cinder+is_neut+is_glance+is_ks+is_api5) > 0 AND failure_class!="unclassified"
| bin _time span=5m aligntime=earliest
| eventstats dc(failure_class) as failure_class_diversity by _time, host
| stats count as fail_n, max(failure_class_diversity) as fcd, first(request_id) as s_req, first(iid) as s_iid, first(src_kind) as s_sk by _time, host, failure_class
| eval nvh=if(failure_class=="no_valid_host", fail_n, 0), ba=if(failure_class=="build_abort", fail_n, 0), rs=if(failure_class=="reschedule", fail_n, 0), ci=if(failure_class=="cinder_attach", fail_n, 0), nu=if(failure_class=="neutron_port", fail_n, 0), gl=if(failure_class=="glance_image", fail_n, 0), ks=if(failure_class=="keystone_auth", fail_n, 0), ap5=if(failure_class=="api_http5xx", fail_n, 0)
| stats sum(fail_n) as total_f, first(fcd) as failure_class_diversity, sum(nvh) as nvh_n, sum(ba) as ba_n, sum(rs) as rs_n, sum(ci) as ci_n, sum(nu) as nu_n, sum(gl) as gl_n, sum(ks) as ks_n, sum(ap5) as ap5_n, values(s_req) as s_req, values(s_iid) as s_iid, values(s_sk) as s_sk by _time, host
| fillnull value=0 nvh_n ba_n rs_n ci_n nu_n gl_n ks_n ap5_n total_f
| sort 0 + _time, host
| eval nvh_bin=if(nvh_n>0,1,0)
| streamstats window=4 global=f current=true sum(nvh_bin) as nvh_bins_flag_last4 by host
| eventstats sum(nvh_n) as fleet_nvh_5m by _time
| eval cinder_neutron=ci_n+nu_n
| eval noisy_host=if(failure_class_diversity>2,1,0)
| eval request_id_sample=if(mvcount(s_req)>0, mvindex(s_req,0), "")
| eval instance_id_sample=if(mvcount(s_iid)>0, mvindex(s_iid,0), "")
| eval src_mode=if(mvcount(s_sk)>0, tostring(mvindex(s_sk,0)), "")
| eval severity_bucket=case((nvh_bins_flag_last4>=3) OR cinder_neutron>=10, "critical", (ba_n>=3) OR (nvh_n>=5) OR (rs_n>=3), "warning", total_f>0, "watch", 1=1, "ok")
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket=="critical",1, severity_bucket=="warning",2, severity_bucket=="watch",3,1=1,4)
| sort 0 sev_order, -total_f, host
| table _time, host, src_mode, nvh_n, ba_n, rs_n, ci_n, nu_n, gl_n, ks_n, ap5_n, total_f, cinder_neutron, nvh_bins_flag_last4, fleet_nvh_5m, failure_class_diversity, noisy_host, request_id_sample, instance_id_sample, severity_bucket, sev_order
description: This use case is the OpenStack Nova build-and-spawn failure detector. It ingests the sourcetype OR described in dataSources, derives src_kind (compute, scheduler, conductor, nova-api umbrella, oslo-filtered JSON, journald) from sourcename, then scores each line with a regex bank: **NoValidHost** and NoMoreHosts-style text for the **nova-scheduler** path, BuildAbortException and failed-to-spawn strings for nova-compute work, RescheduledException-style requeue text, Cinder attach and VolumeNotFound-class strings at boot-from-volume, Neutron port and subnet class failures, Glance image and HTTP error patterns, Keystone 401 and token-class strings, and /v2.1/servers 5xx in API-shaped lines. A coalesce chain covers level, log_level, instance_uuid vs instance vs instance_id, and request_id with a rex fallback for the req- form. A 5m bin with aligntime=earliest plus streamstats approximates recent no_valid_host pressure on a host, and eventstats rolls NoValidHost into fleet_nvh_5m. A four-bucket severity ladder (critical, warning, watch, ok) encodes the thresholds in the spl field. It is not UC-19.1.45 (RabbitMQ mem_alarm, partition, queue) and not UC-19.1.46 (Galera wsrep quorums, SST/IST log lines).
value: Tenants and internal IaaS customers expect create-server to finish in a service-window or charge-back SLO, not to leave instances stuck in BUILD or ERROR with no owner triage. When NoValidHost spikes region-wide, you are in placement-filter or true capacity territory; when a single host shows cinder and neutron classes together, you are often in a hypervisor or local agent fault, not a global design issue. A detector that is explicit about the spawn-stage class shrinks MTTR by routing the page to the Nova platform owner, the Cinder pair, or the Neutron team with evidence, instead of a full-control-plane bridge that UC-19.1.45 and UC-19.1.46 are meant to decompose. The business value is a defensible project- and region-level story: fewer surprise ERROR guest counts in peaks and a cleaner postmortem on provisioning without Rabbit or SQL stories.
implementation: Onboard `/var/log/nova/*.log` and equivalent Kolla paths with UF, align sourcetypes, tune LINE_BREAKER to oslo timestamps, then schedule the `spl` every five minutes with `earliest=-1h`, alert when `severity_bucket` is `critical` or `warning` after a quiet-week baseline, and throttle on `host` for fifteen minutes. Add a `maintenance.csv` or change-system lookup to suppress known drill windows. Optional CMDB join for availability_zone and host_aggregate goes on a child dashboard, not the first alert day.
visualization: Panel A: single value worst `sev_order`. Panel B: timechart of `fleet_nvh_5m` and `total_f` in 5m. Panel C: bar or column chart of failure-class share from a child search that classifies at event layer. Panel D: table of `host`, `src_mode`, `total_f`, `cinder_neutron`, `noisy_host`, `instance_id_sample`, with drill to raw. Optional heatmap: `severity_bucket` by `host` for posture reviews.

references.title: OpenStack Nova — Admin troubleshooting (state, filter, and log triage)
OpenStack Nova — Scheduling, filters, and NoValidHost context
OpenStack Security Guide — introduction and visibility layers
Kolla-Ansible — Nova compute and logging (paths and service layout)
OpenStack Nova release notes (per-release operator changes)
OpenStack add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 2905, archived — historical only)

knownFalsePositives: • A chaos or bug-bash drill that injects NoValidHost or stops a virt layer on purpose under a change ticket, not a tenant-impacting defect. • QA that requests a flavor with extra_specs a lab aggregate cannot satisfy, producing expected no valid host text. • Host-aggregate bring-up where new racks are not yet in the right pool, so the scheduler is honest, not “broken.” • Short Glance read-only failover rehearsal that leaves a few image-class log lines with no long-lived ERROR count in the API. • Ceph or Cinder maintenance with a customer notice where attach strings still appear. • A brief keystone 401 during fernet rotation with sub-minute recovery. • Conductor rolling restarts in order during a canary where reschedule counts dip after steady state. • Non-prod quota overrun experiments that build to failure. • Lab Neutron VLAN re-tag with known binding log noise. • ImageNotAuthorized during an app-cred or keystone exercise that deliberately expires credentials. • A scheduler active/passive test where conductor requeue text appears; pair with a maintenance table before you page, because the Rabbit UC still owns the broker.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
A supported OpenStack release (Antelope through Dalmatian or a vendor LTS) with chrony; Splunk 9.2+ or Splunk Cloud; a search role for `index=openstack`. Before first production use, run five ad-hoc searches per sourcetype and copy real field names into a one-page field map—this is what makes coalesce and rex stable across minor upgrades. Nothing here replaces a Rabbit health UC or a Galera health UC: keep UC-19.1.45 and UC-19.1.46 in the same runbook for brownouts.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

Use deployment-server classes: controller, compute-only, and converged, so a shared log export is not tailed twice. The UF account must be able to read service logs, usually by adding the `splunk` user to a nova-readable group. World-readable permission is a last resort and often blocked by CIS hardening. On Kolla-Ansible, add a second monitor stanza to `/var/log/kolla/nova` or to the path your TripleO / kolla-ansible render uses; never assume `/var/log/nova` exists inside every container when you are bind-mounting from the host.

```ini
[monitor:///var/log/nova/*.log]
disabled = 0
index = openstack
sourcetype = openstack:nova:compute
crcSalt = <SOURCE>

[monitor:///var/log/nova/nova-scheduler.log]
disabled = 0
index = openstack
sourcetype = openstack:nova:scheduler
crcSalt = <SOURCE>

[monitor:///var/log/nova/nova-conductor.log]
disabled = 0
index = openstack
sourcetype = openstack:nova:conductor
crcSalt = <SOURCE>

[monitor:///var/log/nova/nova-api.log]
disabled = 0
index = openstack
sourcetype = openstack:nova:api
crcSalt = <SOURCE>

# Kolla: adjust host segment if your file names are pod-specific
[monitor:///var/log/kolla/nova/*.log*]
disabled = 0
index = openstack
host_segment = 4
sourcetype = openstack:nova
crcSalt = <SOURCE>
```

`props.conf` should merge Python tracebacks, not break them into hundreds of one-line null events. A practical pattern: SHOULD_LINEMERGE true, LINE_BREAKER to the start of a new oslo timestamp, TRUNCATE 0, and a short EXTRACT- for req- and a UUID, then spot-check a failure window in `verbose` mode. If you use JSON oslo, set INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json and map `message` so `coalesce(message,...)` in SPL always finds a msg string. Do not invent sourcetypes you do not have in `| metadata`—archive 2905 is optional history only.

```ini
[openstack:nova]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = true
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)(?=(?:\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}|[0-9\-T:\.Z]{4,}))
TRUNCATE = 0
EXTRACT-nova_req = (?<reqid_tmp>req-(?:[0-9a-fA-F-]{8,64}))

[oslo:json]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
TRUNCATE = 0

[linux:journald]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
# map Unit / SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER if you standardize on journal-only compute images
```

**Step 2 — Build the search**

The `spl` field in this file is the body you paste. It uses a sourcetype OR and src_kind so you can tell API text from compute-side spawns. The failure case order gives no_valid_host strict priority over a generic “failed to spawn” line so one stack trace does not double-count. coalesce on message, `body`, `_raw`, and `payload` covers JSON and file tails; the field-level request IDs and iid are normalised with tostring defensively. The 5m bin plus streamstats on nvh_bin approximates “three of four recent scheduling windows on this host had no_valid_host-class traffic.” cinder + neutron sums drive a 10+ events-in-window critical for mixed storage-network spawn pain. The noisy flag is a simple eventstats `dc` on failure_class in the binned set before the pivot, then >2 in the per-host table—when true, a single compute is throwing multiple independent classes, which often deserves hardware or local agent checks.

```spl
index=openstack (sourcetype="openstack:nova" OR sourcetype="openstack:nova:compute" OR sourcetype="openstack:nova:scheduler" OR sourcetype="openstack:nova:conductor" OR sourcetype="openstack:nova:api" OR sourcetype="oslo:json" OR (sourcetype="linux:journald" (Unit=*nova* OR _raw="*nova-compute*" OR _raw="*nova-scheduler*" OR _raw="*nova-conductor*" OR _raw="*nova-api*" OR _raw="*nova-wsgi*"))) earliest=-1h
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype="openstack:nova:compute","compute", sourcetype="openstack:nova:scheduler","scheduler", sourcetype="openstack:nova:conductor","conductor", sourcetype="openstack:nova:api","api", match(sourcetype,"^openstack:nova$"),"umbrella", sourcetype="oslo:json" AND match(_raw,"(?i)nova(\-|[.])"),"oslo_nova", sourcetype="linux:journald","journald", 1=1,"other")
| eval msg=coalesce(message, body, _raw, msg, payload)
| eval lv=lower(tostring(coalesce(level, log_level, severity, priority, "")))
| eval r1=if(isnotnull(request_id), tostring(request_id), null())
| eval r2=if(isnotnull(requestId), tostring(requestId), null())
| eval r3=if(isnotnull(request_uuid), tostring(request_uuid), null())
| eval iid=tostring(coalesce(instance_uuid, inst_uuid, instance_id, instance, ""))
| rex field=msg max_match=1 "(?<reqid_r>req-[0-9a-fA-F-]{8,64})"
| eval request_id=coalesce(r1, r2, r3, reqid_r, "")
| eval is_nvh=if(match(msg, "(?i)NoValidHost|NoMoreHosts|no valid host|No more (valid )?hosts with capacity"),1,0)
| eval is_build_abort=if(match(msg, "(?i)BuildAbortException|Failed to spawn( instance)?|failed to (build|spawn)|SpawnFailed|BuildError"),1,0)
| eval is_resched=if(match(msg, "(?i)RescheduledException|re-?schedul|rescheduling( exception)?|max scheduling attempts|retry on different host"),1,0)
| eval is_cinder=if(match(msg, "(?i)CinderConnectionFailed|VolumeNotFound|VolumeAttachFailed|volume.*(attach|connection).*fail|is not yet available on the right storage"),1,0)
| eval is_neut=if(match(msg, "(?i)PortBindingFailed|NoSubnetsAvailable|NetworkNotFound|VIF.*failed|Neutron(nova)?(Exception|Error)|binding failed"),1,0)
| eval is_glance=if(match(msg, "(?i)ImageNotAuthorized|ImageNotFound|image.*(inactive|unavailable|403)|Glance.*HTTP\s*5[0-9x]|image.*(fetch|download).*fail"),1,0)
| eval is_ks=if(match(msg, "(?i)HTTP\s*401|Unauthorized(\s*\()?|keystone(\S|\s{1,2})(auth|error)|No valid token|invalid token( cache)?|identity.*(401|unauthorized)"),1,0)
| eval is_api5=if(match(msg, "(?i)(/v2\.1/servers|/os-server-groups).*HTTP\s*5[0-9][0-9]"),1,0)
| eval failure_class=case(is_nvh=1, "no_valid_host", is_build_abort=1, "build_abort", is_resched=1, "reschedule", is_cinder=1, "cinder_attach", is_neut=1, "neutron_port", is_glance=1, "glance_image", is_ks=1, "keystone_auth", is_api5=1, "api_http5xx", 1=1, "unclassified")
| where (is_nvh+is_build_abort+is_resched+is_cinder+is_neut+is_glance+is_ks+is_api5) > 0 AND failure_class!="unclassified"
| bin _time span=5m aligntime=earliest
| eventstats dc(failure_class) as failure_class_diversity by _time, host
| stats count as fail_n, max(failure_class_diversity) as fcd, first(request_id) as s_req, first(iid) as s_iid, first(src_kind) as s_sk by _time, host, failure_class
| eval nvh=if(failure_class=="no_valid_host", fail_n, 0), ba=if(failure_class=="build_abort", fail_n, 0), rs=if(failure_class=="reschedule", fail_n, 0), ci=if(failure_class=="cinder_attach", fail_n, 0), nu=if(failure_class=="neutron_port", fail_n, 0), gl=if(failure_class=="glance_image", fail_n, 0), ks=if(failure_class=="keystone_auth", fail_n, 0), ap5=if(failure_class=="api_http5xx", fail_n, 0)
| stats sum(fail_n) as total_f, first(fcd) as failure_class_diversity, sum(nvh) as nvh_n, sum(ba) as ba_n, sum(rs) as rs_n, sum(ci) as ci_n, sum(nu) as nu_n, sum(gl) as gl_n, sum(ks) as ks_n, sum(ap5) as ap5_n, values(s_req) as s_req, values(s_iid) as s_iid, values(s_sk) as s_sk by _time, host
| fillnull value=0 nvh_n ba_n rs_n ci_n nu_n gl_n ks_n ap5_n total_f
| sort 0 + _time, host
| eval nvh_bin=if(nvh_n>0,1,0)
| streamstats window=4 global=f current=true sum(nvh_bin) as nvh_bins_flag_last4 by host
| eventstats sum(nvh_n) as fleet_nvh_5m by _time
| eval cinder_neutron=ci_n+nu_n
| eval noisy_host=if(failure_class_diversity>2,1,0)
| eval request_id_sample=if(mvcount(s_req)>0, mvindex(s_req,0), "")
| eval instance_id_sample=if(mvcount(s_iid)>0, mvindex(s_iid,0), "")
| eval src_mode=if(mvcount(s_sk)>0, tostring(mvindex(s_sk,0)), "")
| eval severity_bucket=case((nvh_bins_flag_last4>=3) OR cinder_neutron>=10, "critical", (ba_n>=3) OR (nvh_n>=5) OR (rs_n>=3), "warning", total_f>0, "watch", 1=1, "ok")
| eval sev_order=case(severity_bucket=="critical",1, severity_bucket=="warning",2, severity_bucket=="watch",3,1=1,4)
| sort 0 sev_order, -total_f, host
| table _time, host, src_mode, nvh_n, ba_n, rs_n, ci_n, nu_n, gl_n, ks_n, ap5_n, total_f, cinder_neutron, nvh_bins_flag_last4, fleet_nvh_5m, failure_class_diversity, noisy_host, request_id_sample, instance_id_sample, severity_bucket, sev_order
```

**Step 3 — Validate the results**

Take a one-hour window and compare: (1) a fieldsummary on message / level in each sourcetype; (2) a raw tail with req- in msg; (3) openstack server list and openstack server show for a UUID that should appear; (4) a manual check that this UC is not a duplicate of the Erlang Rabbit story (UC-19.1.45) or the mysqld wsrep story (UC-19.1.46), because those siblings answer different on-call playbooks. If fleet_nvh_5m stays flat while the API still returns 500, pivot to the messaging or SQL use cases before you tune regex here. If cinder + neutron is high in one AZ only, a L3 or Ceph change ticket often exists—this UC is still valid class evidence, not the whole root cause.

**Step 4 — Operationalize as alert + dashboard**

Saved search — `cron */5` over -1h, Save then Save as Alert (Settings → Searches, reports, and alerts) when `severity_bucket` in critical or warning; set Suppress 900s on `host` if your fleet is not huge. If you add an email action, pass cinder_neutron and noisy for context. For dashboard layout: a bar of total_f by a failure-class subsearch, a line of fleet_nvh_5m by 5m, and a top-host table of total_f with drill to index=openstack raw by host and _time token. A second “drill” search can be the same `spl` with `| search failure_class!=""` at event layer before the bin if you need per- UUID rows.

**Step 5 — Troubleshoot common issues**

(1) The sourcetype list does not match a new region (typo, vendor rename)—add a single `OR` branch to the base, do not fork three copies of the UC. (2) LINE_BREAKER is too aggressive: tracebacks look like dozens of one-line `ERROR` entries and your is_* flags misfire; relax BREAK to the timestamp head your oslo build prints. (3) A Kolla path moved under /var/log/containers/nova after an upgrade: update monitor `path`, not the SPL. (4) `instance` in JSON is an object, not a string; iid is empty until you add spath or a calculated field. (5) NoValidHost in QA during an aggregate gating test is expected: carry a `change` lookup for suppress. (6) streamstats over sparse 5m bins counts rows, not always four strict clock quads—if you are near zero volume, use `makecontinuous` in a v2, not the first week. (7) Request `req-` is missing: props or rex is wrong. (8) api_http5xx is placement or haproxy in front: cross-check the TCP VIP and wsgi logs, not just nova-api. (9) Journald only: confirm Unit is extracted; if not, widen linux:journald with a string match on `nova-` in `_raw` and accept some noise. (10) Sourcetype is oslo:json but the body is in oslo message; if msg is empty, coalesce order or JSON `FIELD` mapping is wrong. (11) Throttle on host masks a second incident during the same window on that compute—tighten when your estate is <50 nodes. (12) Comparing to Rabbit or Galera UCs, this file can still be green while the region is on fire: brownouts are multi-UC. Use the sibling searches before you “fix” nova regex alone.

For host inventory correlation, a future lookup can map compute host to availability_zone and aggregate name; the base SPL already keys on the host field as the UF sets it. When you add the join, keep the alert on the pre-join table so a missing CMDB row does not hide a real spike. For API vs compute split, you can clone the search and add a src_mode or sourcetype filter in a subsearch if you standardize src_kind before the pivot; the default table uses the first src_mode value as a hint only. Volume: at 5m rollups, a 200-compute region is well within interactive search; if the index grows past tens of GB per day, consider summary indexing on failure_class hits with a 5m granularity, but that is out of scope for day one.
grandmaExplanation: We read the day-to-day factory logs of a private cloud as it builds new virtual machines, and we notice when the scheduling hand-off, the disk or image step, or the last mile on a real server throws the same error again and again. We raise the right internal signal so teams fix a narrow, real cause before every customer queue lights up at once.
hardware: Control-plane KVM guests or 1U/2U x86-64 servers in Caracal-era trains often land at 8–16 vCPU and 32–64 GiB for API, conductor, scheduler, and shared services, on 10/25 Gbps L3 with chrony. Compute is KVM on RHEL/Alma/Rocky or Ubuntu with OVS/OVN, NUMA flavors, and Ceph-backed or NFS-backed Cinder; telco NFV edges are smaller but share the same log story. Kolla and TripleO container layouts still expose /var/log/kolla/nova or an equivalent host path for UF.
